analysis_request.context: EXP06 phantom study. Three signal processing pipelines were applied to ON-state EEG to recover a 12.45 Hz ground-truth (GT) signal during transcranial stimulation. The goal is to understand what transformations were applied at each step, what decisions were made, and what signals were ultimately compared in the figures.

pipeline_overview.input: Raw multichannel EEG from stimulation recording (exp06-STIM-iTBS_run02.vhdr). ON-state windows: 0.3–1.5 s after stimulation onset (per 20 cycles per intensity block). Late-OFF reference windows: 1.5–3.2 s after stimulation offset.

signal_path.label: Raw selected channel
signal_path.step: Bandpass filter all EEG channels into signal band (11.95–12.95 Hz)
Compute PLV against GT channel for each EEG channel
Select channel with highest PLV as output signal
signal_path.output: Best raw EEG channel time series (20 ON epochs × on_window_samples)
signal_path.selection_criterion: Argmax PLV vs GT across all channels

signal_path.label: SASS source
signal_path.step: Bandpass filter ON and late-OFF epochs into view band (4–20 Hz)

SASS: generalized eigendecomposition of cov_A relative to cov_B. Sort components by descending eigenvalue (high λ = artifact-dominated). Null the top N components (N chosen to minimize MSE between projected cov and cov_B). Project back to channel space → artifact-suppressed ON epochs.

Second eigendecomposition on SASS-cleaned data: Bandpass SASS output into signal band; compute cov_ON and cov_OFF. Generalized eigendecomposition: cov_ON · w = λ · cov_OFF · w. Sort ascending (low λ = low ON variance relative to OFF = most suppressed artifact). Keep top 6 components.
Rank 6 components by broadband (4–20 Hz) ON/OFF power ratio. Select component with lowest ratio (most artifact-suppressed).
signal_path.output: One SASS-derived component time series (20 ON epochs × on_window_samples)
signal_path.selection_criterion: Min broadband ON/OFF power ratio from SASS-cleaned data

signal_path.label: SSD component (from raw)
signal_path.step: Bandpass raw ON epochs into signal band (11.95–12.95 Hz) → cov_signal. Bandpass raw ON epochs into view band (4–20 Hz) → cov_view.
Generalized eigendecomposition: cov_signal · w = λ · cov_view · w. Sort descending (high λ = signal-band dominant). Keep top 6 components.
Rank 6 components by broadband (4–20 Hz) ON/OFF power ratio (OFF power from late-OFF raw epochs). Select component with lowest ratio (most artifact-suppressed).
signal_path.output: One SSD component time series from raw data (20 ON epochs × on_window_samples)
signal_path.selection_criterion: Min broadband ON/OFF power ratio from raw data

reference_signal.label: GT vs STIM
reference_signal.description: PLV between the measured stimulation voltage trace and the GT channel. Used as a fixed upper-bound reference — no selection or transformation applied.

decision.rationale: Prior exploratory checks confirmed this is the GT-locked component
decision.note: NOTE: baseline SSD weights are NOT used in analyze_exp06_run02_on_raw_sass_ssd_plv.py — SSD is re-fitted per intensity block on raw ON data

decision.choice: Automatic: MSE criterion between projected covariance and clean reference cov_B
decision.rationale: Finds the number of artifact components whose removal best recovers the clean signal covariance

decision.step: Component ranking for SASS source and SSD
decision.rationale: Proxy for artifact suppression — component with least ON-state broadband power inflation relative to OFF is presumed most artifact-free
decision.note: Raw channel uses PLV directly; SASS and SSD use power ratio — asymmetric criteria

compared_signals.signal: SSD component (signal-band eigendecomp on raw → power-ratio ranked)
compared_signals.metric: PLV against GT channel, per intensity block (10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%)
